sanskrit:
यच्चापि सर्वभूतानां बीजं तदहमर्जुन।
न तदस्ति विना यत्स्यान्मया भूतं चराचरम्॥
transliteration:
yac cāpi sarva-bhūtānāṃ bījaṃ tad aham arjuna |
na tad asti vinā yat syān mayā bhūtaṃ carācaram ||
literal_meaning: Whatever the seed of all beings, that I am, O Arjuna; without Me nothing can exist—moving or unmoving.
contextual_meaning: Krishna asserts that every psychological impulse, thought, or identity is rooted in a single conscious source; recognizing this collapses the illusion of independent self-existence and relieves the burden of personal authorship.
psychological_state: Cognitive restructuring from personal agency to participatory consciousness; shift from 'I must control' to 'I am already held'.
trigger_scenario: A CEO having panic attacks realizes every decision emerges from a field of intelligence larger than her persona and begins to delegate without shame.
anchor_text: Every thought and feeling flows from one conscious source; you are its witness, not its author.
